name: Fiesta Pawn Inc
description_short: FirstCash is the leading international operator of pawn stores with 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, the UK, and Latin America, offering pawn loans secured by personal property and retail merchandise sales.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc., headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas, is the largest pawn shop operator globally with over 3,300 retail locations spanning 29 U.S. states, the District of Columbia, the United Kingdom, Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador. The company employs approximately 22,000 people and is listed on both the S&P MidCap 400 Index and Russell 2000 Index, indicating substantial scale and financial stability in the consumer finance sector.

FirstCash's core business model focuses on serving cash and credit-constrained consumers through two primary services: pawn loans and retail merchandise sales. The company makes small non-recourse pawn loans secured by pledged personal property (no credit check required) and buys/sells a wide variety of items including jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments. Additionally, through its wholly-owned subsidiary AFF, FirstCash provides lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions across a network of 15,000+ retail merchant partners.

What distinguishes FirstCash is its massive geographic footprint and operational scale—significantly larger than independent pawn shops. The company offers additional services like gold/precious metal buying, layaway plans with 10% down, and a robust store locator network. Their pawn loans are non-recourse, meaning customers only lose the pledged item if they default, not personal assets. The subsidiary AFF adds a technology-driven payment solutions component targeting the same consumer demographic.

The honest assessment: FirstCash serves a genuine market need for fast, no-credit-check cash access, but pawn loans inherently come with the risk of losing collateral. Customers must be comfortable with this trade-off. The company's scale provides convenience and consistency, but pawn rates and terms vary by location. This is best suited for consumers who have valuable items they're willing to pledge and need immediate cash without credit checks.
pros: 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states plus international presence ensures widespread accessibility and convenience
Non-recourse pawn loans—customers only lose pledged items, not personal assets, if they default
No credit check required; instant cash funding available with minimal documentation
Buys and sells diverse merchandise (jewelry, electronics, tools, instruments) providing both borrowing and shopping options
Subsidiary AFF provides lease-to-own and retail financing through 15,000+ merchant partners, expanding product reach
S&P MidCap 400 and Russell 2000 Index component indicates financial stability and regulatory oversight
Layaway service available with affordable 10% down payments for budget-conscious consumers
Gold and precious metal buying at competitive cash-on-the-spot rates
cons: Risk of losing collateral if pawn loan is not repaid by the deadline, creating financial loss beyond just interest
Pawn loan terms and interest rates vary by location; no standardized nationwide rates disclosed on website
Limited transparency on specific interest rates, loan terms, and repayment periods in website content
Non-recourse structure may incentivize aggressive lending practices or high rates that exploit financially desperate consumers
Merchandise inventory from pawned items raises questions about whether customers receive fair valuations for pledged goods
best_for: Consumers with valuable collateral (jewelry, electronics, instruments) who need emergency cash within hours
Individuals with poor or no credit who cannot qualify for traditional personal loans or credit-based financing
People seeking fast cash for short-term needs who can repay within weeks without facing excessive interest accumulation
Shoppers looking to purchase second-hand merchandise or lay away items with minimal upfront payment
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for cash-constrained consumers with valuable collateral who need immediate funding without credit checks and can repay within the specified loan term. The primary caveat is that defaulting results in loss of the pledged item—customers must be comfortable with this collateral risk and should carefully evaluate whether they can meet repayment deadlines before pawning valuable possessions.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property (jewelry, electronics, tools, instruments, appliances, sporting goods)
Gold, silver, and platinum buying at cash-on-the-spot rates
Retail sales of second-hand merchandise across multiple categories
Layaway service with 10% down payment option
Lease-to-own financing through AFF subsidiary (15,000+ merchant partners)
Retail finance payment solutions for consumer goods and services
Store locator service to find nearest pawn location
Non-recourse pawn loan structure (collateral-based, not credit-based)
Wide merchandise acquisition (buy almost anything of value)
International pawn services across Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, El Salvador, and UK
meta_description: FirstCash operates 3,300+ pawn stores across 29 U.S. states, the UK, and Latin America, offering pawn loans and retail merchandise.
